extraction.one_line_summary: BMC won the TTT by 4 seconds over Mitchelton-Scott, with DENNIS driving the line to secure the margin in the final 50 meters.
tactical_patterns: DENNIS positioned on front for BMC's final push demonstrates sprinter-led TTT finishes preserve GC riders for selective stages ahead.
Mitchelton-Scott's unexpected speed (22:23) suggests hidden TTT strength that makes them dark horses for team-dependent stages this season.
KELDERMAN maintained position with only 5 finishers, revealing Sunweb accepts rider losses to preserve core GC group's time competitiveness.
Sky's 9-second deficit despite FROOME/THOMAS presence signals their TTT decline creates exploitable GC vulnerability in stage race openings.

rider_signals.MEINTJES Louis: negative: finished 1:07 down in group of four, far from ideal and signals early GC time loss vulnerability
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: neutral: UAE posted respectable 23:04, on parity with education first, suggests steady but unremarkable early form
rider_signals.MAJKA Rafał: positive: finished 30 seconds down at 22:49, making big gains on other GC riders like Kelderman and Nibali
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: neutral: 22:49 sixth place is not bad at all, only 30 seconds down shows solid early positioning
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: neutral: maintained classification position with only 5 finishers, improved relative to Sunweb despite rider losses
